name: Elaine Alvarez
text: I spent so many a day studying here, rather than the smaller Health Science Library. The upper levels provide plenty of desks with stalls that are conductive to studying. On quiet days, one can hear the bell tower every 15 minutes from here.
address: Davis Library, 208 Raleigh St, Chapel Hill, NC 27514
title: Davis Library

name: Sydney Norman
text: Doesn’t have a coffee shop. Always crowded. Always dirty. Bathrooms are a sin.
address: Davis Library, 208 Raleigh St, Chapel Hill, NC 27514
title: Davis Library

name: Jeidy Vega
text: Really nice place to come, do research and homework, work virtually or just to sit. It’s a silent and quiet place. Very well organized, with COVID-19 protocol and 6ft apart, there is also a security guard inside the building that makes the place even more safer.
address: Davis Library, 208 Raleigh St, Chapel Hill, NC 27514
title: Davis Library

name: Tiffanie Lee
text: Popular study spot for students. Great staff and programming- offered coffee and wellness activities during midterms/finals. I would recommend taking the elevator during the summer because you might end up locked in a staircase otherwise.
address: Davis Library, 208 Raleigh St, Chapel Hill, NC 27514
title: Davis Library

name: Ella Carter
text: Nice place to hang out and study with friends. They have free office supplies, printers, and scanners for students. The top floors are very quiet, and the bottom 2 floors are lowder with nice, comfy furniture. I love this place
address: Davis Library, 208 Raleigh St, Chapel Hill, NC 27514

name: grace north
text: Yas one of my favorite libraries on campus. I like how it gets quieter with each floor so you have areas for talking and areas for focused work. The elevator is kind of bunk but I really love the windows on the back wall so pretty!
address: Davis Library, 208 Raleigh St, Chapel Hill, NC 27514
title: Davis Library

name: Ben
address: Davis Library, 208 Raleigh St, Chapel Hill, NC 27514
title: Davis Library

name: Gabe
text:
I don't know what was going on with the folks working at the library, but it was enough to never make me want to come back here again.

Went here to take an exam in peace and quiet. At some point a noisy crowd came in and were practically taking up the whole main hall with how loud they were talking, but the front desk staff said nothing to them. Halfway through my test, there was an announcement that the library would close within 30 minutes. No worries, it's on me and I should have checked the time before I started. I packed up my stuff and tried to open the door 25 minutes to closing only to realize the doors were already locked.

There wasn't a staff member in sight. I had to wander around the library until I finally found someone. When I asked them to let me out of the building, it was as if I had asked them to lend me money. They eventually, begrudgingly let me out. I'd advise you to steer clear if you're looking for a place to study in peace.
address: Davis Library, 208 Raleigh St, Chapel Hill, NC 27514
title: Davis Library

name: Yelena P. Francis
text: It is a very good library, well-organized, and the people working there are efficient and friendly. They have a good Russian language collection of books.
address: Davis Library, 208 Raleigh St, Chapel Hill, NC 27514
title: Davis Library

name: Zach Hibbard
text: Great place to study, the group study rooms are very helpful if you can manage to reserve one in time. I'd recommend getting your spot at least two weeks ahead of the start of finals season, these rooms fill up very very fast. Also, the first and second floors provide a very laid back study atmosphere for anyone who doesn't like the ultra quiet 8th floor.
address: Davis Library, 208 Raleigh St, Chapel Hill, NC 27514
title: Davis Library

title: Davis Library

name: Amalia Mammadova
text: Two stars for the 8th floor, otherwise, no stars. Lol. This library really needs to be updated. Stained ripped/up carpet, broken furniture, writing all over the walls and study cubicles, and disgusting spider webs on the windows? Come on. This is supposed to be the main library on campus. Lol.
responseFromOwnerText: We're sorry you had a bad experience visiting Davis. If you can email or chat us specifics of where you saw the problems, we're happy to look into it: https://library.unc.edu/ask/

name: Nathaniel Zhang
text: 工作人员非常热情，图书馆很安静，很舒适，没有异味。工作效率很高，WIFI 速度也很快。
textTranslated: The staff were very welcoming, the library was quiet and comfortable, and there were no unpleasant odors. The work efficiency was high, and the Wi-Fi speed was also very fast.
address: Davis Library, 208 Raleigh St, Chapel Hill, NC 27514
title: Davis Library

name: EC
text: Eight levels, and dozens of rows of books, hundreds of chairs, and study holes... This library is an absolute gem, and there's tons of books to be discovered here. This is the place you can go for reference books, and while it doesn't have as much new tech as the UL, it's really a good atmosphere for studying and working on projects. For anyone who has read the Name of the Wind, you'll know this is basically the archives... great stuff!
address: Davis Library, 208 Raleigh St, Chapel Hill, NC 27514
